commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-wiki unsafe-local sync to detect unreadable configured paths or nested directories, skip imported-source pruning during those outages, and add regression coverage for transient outages plus readable-directory deletion.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +82. Total +116 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-backed. The linked issue includes a production-function current-main repro, and current main still drops missing configured paths before the shared prune deletes inactive unsafe-local entries.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor real behavior proof is still missing, and the remaining prune-scope compatibility decision needs author or maintainer revision before merge.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): don't prune unsafe-local pages when a source path is   transiently unavailable
target.body:
Closes #97523

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where memory-wiki users running `vaultMode: "unsafe-local"` would permanently lose imported pages **and their hand-written human-notes blocks** when a configured source path became transiently unavailable (an undocked external drive, a rebooting NAS, or a cloud-synced folder that is not mounted yet). Any `wiki_*` tool that triggered a sync while the path was gone deleted every imported page for that path; when the path came back, pages were recreated empty and the user's notes were unrecoverable.

## Why This Change Was Made

A transiently unreadable source collects zero artifacts in exactly the same way a real deletion does, and the `unsafe-local` sync pruned unconditionally on that signal. `collectUnsafeLocalArtifacts` now reports when a configured path could not be read — `fs.stat` failing on the path itself, or `fs.readdir` failing on the configured directory **or any nested subdirectory** (the recursive readability now propagates up) — and `syncMemoryWikiUnsafeLocalSources` skips `pruneImportedSourceEntries` whenever that happens, so nothing is deleted until every configured source is readable again. This mirrors the existing capability-gated prune guard already used by the bridge-sync path (#68373). Genuine deletions (a file removed from a directory that is still readable) continue to prune as before.

This intentionally takes the conservative, data-safety-first position: when any configured source (or nested mount) is unreadable, all unsafe-local pruning is skipped for that sync. Stale pages from a real deletion may linger one extra sync during an unrelated outage, which is preferable to deleting a user's notes; they self-heal on the next clean sync.

## User Impact

Users with `unsafe-local` notes on removable, network, or cloud-synced storage no longer lose imported pages or their manually authored human-notes when a source path (or a nested mount inside it) is briefly offline. After the source returns, the next sync reconciles cleanly with notes intact. No config changes required.

## Evidence

After-fix behavior on this branch — `pnpm test extensions/memory-wiki/src/unsafe-local.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`:

```
 ✓ imports explicit private paths and preserves unsafe-local provenance
 ✓ prunes stale unsafe-local pages when a file inside a readable directory is deleted
 ✓ keeps imported pages and human notes when a configured directory is transiently unreadable
 ✓ does not prune when a nested subdirectory is transiently unreadable
 ✓ caps composed unsafe-local filenames to the filesystem component limit

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)
```

What the new/updated tests prove:

- `keeps imported pages and human notes when a configured directory is transiently unreadable` — imports a page, writes a real user note into its human-notes block, takes the source directory offline with a real `fs.rename` outage, syncs (asserts `removedCount` 0 and the page + note survive), then re-mounts and syncs again (clean, note still intact).
- `does not prune when a nested subdirectory is transiently unreadable` — covers the ClawSweeper finding at `unsafe-local.ts`: a nested subdirectory made unreadable mid-sync no longer prunes (`removedCount` 0).
- `prunes stale unsafe-local pages when a file inside a readable directory is deleted` — confirms genuine deletions inside a still-readable directory still prune (`removedCount` 1).

oxlint and `oxfmt --check` are clean on both changed files.

> Note: the local full extension lane shows 4 failures in `source-sync-state.test.ts` / `doctor-contract-api.test.ts` with `TypeError: statement.columns is not a function`. These reproduce identically on a clean checkout with my changes stashed; they are a `node:sqlite` incompatibility with local Node 23 (non-LTS) and are unrelated to this change. CI runs on Linux Node 24.

AI-assisted (Claude Code); change reviewed and understood by the author.
target.author: lexes7
